user.title: Connect EZVIZ HP7 / CP7
user.description: Enter your EZVIZ credentials and region.

pick_serial.title: Select Device
pick_serial.description: Choose the serial number of your HP7 / CP7.

enter_serial.title: Enter Serial Number
enter_serial.description: Manually enter the serial number of your HP7 / CP7.

sms.title: SMS Verification
sms.description: Enter the SMS code EZVIZ sent you to complete two-factor authentication.

unlock_gate.name: Unlock gate
unlock_gate.description: Unlock the gate via EZVIZ cloud.

unlock_door.name: Unlock door
unlock_door.description: Unlock the door via EZVIZ cloud.

init.title: EZVIZ HP7 / CP7 options
init.description: Enter the indoor monitor serial (or several serials separated by commas for the HP7 two-family setup) to expose a dedicated switch per monitor. Empty = no monitor.

data.relay_port: Fixed TCP port for the live relay (0 = random, suggested 8554 for go2rtc / mediamtx)
data.relay_bind: Relay listen host (127.0.0.1 = local-only, safe default; 0.0.0.0 exposes the UNAUTHENTICATED raw stream to other hosts, e.g. Frigate — trusted networks only, requires a fixed port)
data.aggressive_mpegts: Aggressive MPEG-TS compatibility (enable if you see 'Immediate exit requested' in logs; safe to leave OFF by default)
data.video_codec: Video codec (auto = detect; hevc transcodes H.265 to H.264 for WebRTC; hevc_copy passes H.265 through untouched for low-power hosts / Frigate / Safari)
data.stream_source: Stream source (cloud = EZVIZ VTM relay; local = direct LAN, bypasses the cloud and fixes 'no live' on firmware that won't push to the cloud — needs HA on the same network as the doorbell; auto = try local then cloud)
data.stream_mode: Live view delivery (auto = default: detects the codec and picks for you — H.264 → webrtc, HEVC → mjpeg; webrtc = HA Stream/go2rtc, has audio + low latency but can't show HEVC without transcoding; mjpeg = per-viewer ffmpeg to motion-JPEG, codec-agnostic and robust for HEVC/multi-viewer but no audio)

hevc_webrtc.title: HEVC live view on WebRTC mode
hevc_webrtc.description:
Doorbell {serial} streams HEVC/H.265, but Stream mode is set to WebRTC. Browsers can't show HEVC over WebRTC and go2rtc must transcode, which fails on weak hosts (grey screen or 'connection refused').

Open Configure on the integration and set Stream mode to 'mjpeg' (codec-agnostic, no go2rtc), then Reload. Alternatively, to keep WebRTC, set Video codec to 'hevc' to transcode to H.264.
